query:
You are working on the wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 23.01 (commit: 361bc30a).

## Issue to Resolve

CompletionHandlerException: Exception in resume onCancellation handler for CancellableContinuation(DispatchedContinuation[Dis...
Sentry Issue: [JETPACK-ANDROID-92S](https://a8c.sentry.io/issues/4295575735/?referrer=github_integration)

```
IllegalStateException: This mutex is not locked
    at kotlinx.coroutines.sync.MutexImpl.unlock(Mutex.kt:207)
    at kotlinx.coroutines.sync.MutexImpl$CancellableContinuationWithOwner$tryResume$token$1.invoke(Mutex.kt:255)
    at kotlinx.coroutines.sync.MutexImpl$CancellableContinuationWithOwner$tryResume$token$1.invoke(Mutex.kt:252)
    at kotlinx.coroutines.CancellableContinuationImpl.callOnCancellation(CancellableContinuationImpl.kt:259)
    at kotlinx.coroutines.CompletedContinuation.invokeHandlers(CancellableContinuationImpl.kt:659)
...
(8 additional frame(s) were not displayed)

CompletionHandlerException: Exception in resume onCancellation handler for CancellableContinuation(DispatchedContinuation[Dispatchers.IO, Continuation at org.wordpress.android.ui.uploads.UploadStarter$upload$2.invokeSuspend(UploadStarter.kt:211)@df58903]){Completed}@c9c7080
    at kotlinx.coroutines.CancellableContinuationImpl.callOnCancellation(CancellableContinuationImpl.kt:264)
    at kotlinx.coroutines.CompletedContinuation.invokeHandlers(CancellableContinuationImpl.kt:659)
    at kotlinx.coroutines.CancellableContinuationImpl.cancelCompletedResult$kotlinx_coroutines_core(CancellableContinuationImpl.kt:180)
    at kotlinx.coroutines.DispatchedTask.run(DispatchedTask.kt:100)
    at kotlinx.coroutines.internal.LimitedDispatcher$Worker.run(LimitedDispatcher.kt:115)
...
(5 additional frame(s) were not displayed)
```

This is not a duplicate of #17463 since this is now happening on coroutines 1.7.1 which fixed the underlying root cause of that other ticket. This has a similar stacktrace, but it looks like we might be misusing the Mutex in our `UploadStarter` implementation.

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

**TLDR:**Â I think our code is silently making the crash look like itâ€™s an internal Mutex problem but in reality, we might be callingÂ `unlock`Â unnecessarily when the Mutex internals are not expecting it. We should removeÂ [this line](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L186)Â and IÂ thinkÂ it might solve our issues on coroutines 1.7.1.

Based on theÂ [breadcrumbs](https://a8c.sentry.io/issues/4295575735/?project=5731682&referrer=issue-stream&sort=freq&statsPeriod=14d&stream_index=1#breadcrumbs) I believe the actual issue is that we tried toÂ [start the post upload service](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L175-L179)Â while the app was in the background and the system didnâ€™t let us, which caused allÂ `upload`Â jobs (createdÂ [here](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L138-L142)) to be canceled since theÂ [Parent Job](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L68) is not aÂ `SupervisorJob`.

This made all jobs (except the first one which failed because of anÂ `IllegalStateException`)Â [fall here](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L183-L187)Â (as can be seen in the breadcrumb logs).

**What I first thought could be happening is:**

We use theÂ `bgDispatcher`Â ([Dispatchers.Default](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/e03d208247c7487a7497f873d91f6b0e52a3622e/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/modules/ThreadModule.kt#L40-L42)) which has several threads in the pool and we use the sameÂ `mutex`Â for all upload jobs. Because of that, multiple coroutines (running in different threads) are suspended awaiting the toÂ [acquire](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Semaphore.kt#L196-L201C6)Â the semaphore (whichÂ [creates a queue of segments](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Semaphore.kt#L285-L315)).

Since multiple jobs are being canceled at once, IÂ thinkÂ that the cancellation was not able to be properly propagated and theÂ `segment`Â state for one of them was not marked asÂ `CANCELLED`Â making theÂ `Semaphore#tryResumeNextFromQueue`Â ([which isÂ called on](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/1.7.1/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Semaphore.kt#L265)Â `Semaphore#release`Â [via the](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Mutex.kt#L216)Â `Mutex#unlock`)Â [call the](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Semaphore.kt#L329-L341)Â `tryResumeAcquire`. Which in turnÂ [calls](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Semaphore.kt#L347C23-L347C23)Â `Mutex#tryResume`Â but since that is canceled it runs theÂ `onCancellation`Â [callback](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Mutex.kt#L253-L255)Â when trying to resume the underlying continuation, which isÂ [where the crash is happening](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Mutex.kt#L250-L262).

But I am not 100% sure about that, as I feel like this scenario would be pretty common for a synchronization mechanism using coroutines (like `Mutex` and `Semaphore`), which is why I believe thatÂ `onCancellation`Â callback alsoÂ unlocksÂ the `Mutex`, in case of that late propagation of the cancellation to the mutexÂ `Segment`.

**So, considering that the code makes sense and works (hopefully) what IÂ thinkÂ might be the issue is:**

We also have aÂ [call to](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L186)Â `unlock`Â on our sideÂ and from the breadcrumbs we know that the code is reaching that line. So it might be that thisÂ `unlock`Â is actually unlocking the mutexÂ **at the same time as**Â the whole cancellation flow I mentioned above is happening (_which would be possible because of the multiple threads in the Dispatcher pool_), so when the cancelation reaches theÂ `onCancellation`Â [callback here](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Mutex.kt#L253-L255) our code already unlocked it, causing the crash to happen in theÂ `Mutex`Â internals.

This is my best guess, but the Mutex/Semaphore code is a bit complex and I didnâ€™t fully understand it yet (and probably never willÂ ðŸ˜†).

### Suggestion
Remove that `unlock`Â [call ASAP](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L186)Â as we really donâ€™t need it since we useÂ `withLock`Â which is better in 2 ways:
1. It makes sure toÂ `unlock`Â when exceptions happen ([try/finally](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Mutex.kt#L120-L131))
2. It only callsÂ `unlock`Â if it was successfully locked before running theÂ `action`, so if my hypothesis above is correct, the internalÂ `lockSuspend`Â code will cause a cancellation before reaching thatÂ `try/finally`Â block

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

conversation_history.content:
1â†’package org.wordpress.android.ui.uploads
  2â†’
  3â†’import android.content.Context
  4â†’import androidx.lifecycle.DefaultLifecycleObserver
  5â†’import androidx.lifecycle.LifecycleOwner
  6â†’import androidx.lifecycle.LiveData
  7â†’import androidx.lifecycle.Observer
  8â†’import androidx.lifecycle.ProcessLifecycleOwner
  9â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.CancellationException
 10â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineDispatcher
 11â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope
 12â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.Job
 13â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.async
 14â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.coroutineScope
 15â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.launch
 16â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.sync.Mutex
 17â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.sync.withLock
 18â†’import org.wordpress.android.analytics.AnalyticsTracker.Stat
 19â†’import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.Dispatcher
 20â†’import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.generated.UploadActionBuilder
 21â†’import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.SiteModel
 22â†’import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PageStore
 23â†’import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostStore
 24â†’import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.SiteStore
 25â†’import org.wordpress.android.modules.BG_THREAD
 26â†’import org.wordpress.android.modules.IO_THREAD
 27â†’import org.wordpress.android.testing.OpenForTesting
 28â†’import org.wordpress.android.ui.uploads.UploadActionUseCase.UploadAction
 29â†’import org.wordpress.android.ui.uploads.UploadActionUseCase.UploadAction.DO_NOTHING
 30â†’import org.wordpress.android.util.AppLog
 31â†’import org.wordpress.android.util.AppLog.T
 32â†’import org.wordpress.android.util.NetworkUtilsWrapper
 33â†’import org.wordpress.android.util.analytics.AnalyticsTrackerWrapper
 34â†’import org.wordpress.android.util.skip
 35â†’import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.helpers.ConnectionStatus
 36â†’import javax.inject.Inject
 37â†’import javax.inject.Named
 38â†’import javax.inject.Singleton
 39â†’import kotlin.coroutines.CoroutineContext
 40â†’
 41â†’/**
 42â†’ * Automatically remote-auto-save or upload all local modifications to posts.
 43â†’ *
 44â†’ * Auto-uploads happen when the app is placed in the foreground or when the internet connection is restored. In
 45â†’ * addition to this, call sites can also request an immediate execution by calling [checkConnectionAndUpload].
 46â†’ *
 47â†’ * The method [activateAutoUploading] must be called once, preferably during app creation, for the auto-uploads to work.
 48â†’ */
 49â†’@Singleton
 50â†’@OpenForTesting
 51â†’class UploadStarter @Inject constructor(
 52â†’    /**
 53â†’     * The Application context
 54â†’     */
 55â†’    private val context: Context,
 56â†’    private val dispatcher: Dispatcher,
 57â†’    private val postStore: PostStore,
 58â†’    private val pageStore: PageStore,
 59â†’    private val siteStore: SiteStore,
 60â†’    private val uploadActionUseCase: UploadActionUseCase,
 61â†’    private val tracker: AnalyticsTrackerWrapper,
 62â†’    @Named(BG_THREAD) private val bgDispatcher: CoroutineDispatcher,
 63â†’    @Named(IO_THREAD) private val ioDispatcher: CoroutineDispatcher,
 64â†’    private val uploadServiceFacade: UploadServiceFacade,
 65â†’    private val networkUtilsWrapper: NetworkUtilsWrapper,
 66â†’    private val connectionStatus: LiveData<ConnectionStatus>
 67â†’) : CoroutineScope {
 68â†’    private val job = Job()
 69â†’
 70â†’    /**
 71â†’     * When the app comes to foreground both `queueUploadFromAllSites` and `queueUploadFromSite` are invoked.
 72â†’     * The problem is that they can run in parallel and `uploadServiceFacade.isPostUploadingOrQueued(it)` might return
 73â†’     * out-of-date result and a same post is added twice.
 74â†’     */
 75â†’    private val mutex = Mutex()
 76â†’
 77â†’    override val coroutineContext: CoroutineContext get() = job + bgDispatcher
 78â†’
 79â†’    /**
 80â†’     * The hook for making this class automatically launch uploads whenever the app is placed in the foreground.
 81â†’     */
 82â†’    private val processLifecycleObserver = object : DefaultLifecycleObserver {
 83â†’        override fun onStart(owner: LifecycleOwner) {
 84â†’            queueUploadFromAllSites()
 85â†’        }
 86â†’    }
 87â†’
 88â†’    /**
 89â†’     * Activates the necessary observers for this class to start auto-uploading.
 90â†’     *
 91â†’     * This must be called during [org.wordpress.android.WordPress]' creation like so:
 92â†’     *
 93â†’     * ```
 94â†’     * mUploadStarter.activateAutoUploading(ProcessLifecycleOwner.get())
 95â†’     * ```
 96â†’     */
 97â†’    fun activateAutoUploading(processLifecycleOwner: ProcessLifecycleOwner) {
 98â†’        // We're skipping the first emitted value because the processLifecycleObserver below will also trigger an
 99â†’        // immediate upload.
100â†’        connectionStatus.skip(1).observe(processLifecycleOwner, Observer {
101â†’            queueUploadFromAllSites()
102â†’        })
103â†’
104â†’        processLifecycleOwner.lifecycle.addObserver(processLifecycleObserver)
105â†’    }
106â†’
107â†’    fun queueUploadFromAllSites() = launch {
108â†’        val sites = siteStore.sites
109â†’        try {
110â†’            checkConnectionAndUpload(sites = sites)
111â†’        } catch (e: Exception) {
112â†’            AppLog.e(T.MEDIA, e)
113â†’        }
114â†’    }
115â†’
116â†’    /**
117â†’     * Upload all local drafts from the given [site].
118â†’     */
119â†’    fun queueUploadFromSite(site: SiteModel) = launch {
120â†’        try {
121â†’            checkConnectionAndUpload(sites = listOf(site))
122â†’        } catch (e: Exception) {
123â†’            AppLog.e(T.MEDIA, e)
124â†’        }
125â†’    }
126â†’
127â†’    /**
128â†’     * If there is an internet connection, uploads all posts with local changes belonging to [sites].
129â†’     *
130â†’     * This coroutine will suspend until all the [upload] operations have completed. If one of them fails, all query
131â†’     * and queuing attempts ([upload]) will be canceled. The exception will be thrown by this method.
132â†’     */
133â†’    private suspend fun checkConnectionAndUpload(sites: List<SiteModel>) = coroutineScope {
134â†’        if (!networkUtilsWrapper.isNetworkAvailable()) {
135â†’            return@coroutineScope
136â†’        }
137â†’
138â†’        sites.forEach {
139â†’            launch(ioDispatcher) {
140â†’                upload(site = it)
141â†’            }
142â†’        }
143â†’    }
144â†’
145â†’    /**
146â†’     * This is meant to be used by [checkConnectionAndUpload] only.
147â†’     */
148â†’    private suspend fun upload(site: SiteModel) = coroutineScope {
149â†’        try {
150â†’            mutex.withLock {
151â†’                val posts = async { postStore.getPostsWithLocalChanges(site) }
152â†’                val pages = async { pageStore.getPagesWithLocalChanges(site) }
153â†’                val list = posts.await() + pages.await()
154â†’
155â†’                list.asSequence()
156â†’                    .map { post ->
157â†’                        val action = uploadActionUseCase.getAutoUploadAction(post, site)
158â†’                        Pair(post, action)
159â†’                    }
160â†’                    .filter { (_, action) ->
161â†’                        action != DO_NOTHING
162â†’                    }
163â†’                    .toList()
164â†’                    .forEach { (post, action) ->
165â†’                        trackAutoUploadAction(action, post.status, post.isPage)
166â†’                        AppLog.d(
167â†’                            AppLog.T.POSTS,
168â†’                            "UploadStarter for post (isPage: ${post.isPage}) title: ${post.title}, action: $action"
169â†’                        )
170â†’                        dispatcher.dispatch(
171â†’                            UploadActionBuilder.newIncrementNumberOfAutoUploadAttemptsAction(
172â†’                                post
173â†’                            )
174â†’                        )
175â†’                        uploadServiceFacade.uploadPost(
176â†’                            context = context,
177â†’                            post = post,
178â†’                            trackAnalytics = false
179â†’                        )
180â†’                    }
181â†’            }
182â†’        } catch (e: CancellationException) {
183â†’            AppLog.e(T.MEDIA, e)
184â†’            // Do any needed actions while we are still holding the mutex lock, then release it and rethrow the
185â†’            // exception so it can be handled upstream
186â†’            mutex.unlock()
187â†’            throw e
188â†’        }
189â†’    }
190â†’
191â†’    private fun trackAutoUploadAction(
192â†’        action: UploadAction,
193â†’        status: String,
194â†’        isPage: Boolean
195â†’    ) {
196â†’        tracker.track(
197â†’            if (isPage) Stat.AUTO_UPLOAD_PAGE_INVOKED else Stat.AUTO_UPLOAD_POST_INVOKED,
198â†’            mapOf(
199â†’                "upload_action" to action.toString(),
200â†’                "post_status" to status
201â†’            )
202â†’        )
203â†’    }
204â†’}
205â†’

content.text:
Now I understand the issue. The problem is on line 186 where `mutex.unlock()` is being called manually inside a `CancellationException` catch block. However, the `mutex.withLock { ... }` used on line 150 already handles unlocking automatically through its try/finally mechanism, even when exceptions occur.

When a cancellation happens:
1. The cancellation exception is thrown inside the `withLock` block
2. `withLock`'s internal finally block automatically unlocks the mutex
3. The exception propagates to the catch block
4. The catch block calls `mutex.unlock()` again on line 186
5. This causes the "IllegalStateException: This mutex is not locked" crash

The fix is to remove the manual `mutex.unlock()` call since `withLock` already handles this properly.

 41â†’/**
 42â†’ * Automatically remote-auto-save or upload all local modifications to posts.
 43â†’ *
 44â†’ * Auto-uploads happen when the app is placed in the foreground or when the internet connection is restored. In
 45â†’ * addition to this, call sites can also request an immediate execution by calling [checkConnectionAndUpload].
 46â†’ *
 47â†’ * The method [activateAutoUploading] must be called once, preferably during app creation, for the auto-uploads to work.
 48â†’ */
 49â†’@Singleton
 50â†’@OpenForTesting
 51â†’class UploadStarter @Inject constructor(
 52â†’    /**
 53â†’     * The Application context
 54â†’     */
 55â†’    private val context: Context,

 63â†’    @Named(IO_THREAD) private val ioDispatcher: CoroutineDispatcher,
 64â†’    private val uploadServiceFacade: UploadServiceFacade,
 65â†’    private val networkUtilsWrapper: NetworkUtilsWrapper,
 66â†’    private val connectionStatus: LiveData<ConnectionStatus>
 67â†’) : CoroutineScope {
 68â†’    private val job = Job()
 69â†’
 70â†’    /**
 71â†’     * When the app comes to foreground both `queueUploadFromAllSites` and `queueUploadFromSite` are invoked.
 72â†’     * The problem is that they can run in parallel and `uploadServiceFacade.isPostUploadingOrQueued(it)` might return
 73â†’     * out-of-date result and a same post is added twice.
 74â†’     */
 75â†’    private val mutex = Mutex()
 76â†’

 81â†’     */

 83â†’        override fun onStart(owner: LifecycleOwner) {

 85â†’        }
 86â†’    }
 87â†’
